mechanism_narrative: Sustained high unemployment directly depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to broad market declines over long horizons.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the 60-day forward return of SPX is positive in more than 50% of periods when UNRATE is above its historical median.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance that raises the discount rate on future earnings, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: In the 20 trading days following FEDFUNDS rising above 4.5%, the SPX yields positive returns in more than 40% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth outpaces industrial production growth over a 3-month period, it signals demand overheating relative to supply capacity, stoking inflationary pressures that lead to negative equity returns as markets price in tightening.
candidate_trigger: 3-month growth of RSAFS exceeds 3-month growth of INDPRO by more than 1 percentage point
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition holds, the 20-day forward return of SPX is positive more than 45% of the time, or the average return is not negative (one-tailed test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of very high unemployment (UNRATE above 8%), increases in nonfarm payrolls signal genuine economic recovery and are strongly positive for equity markets. This effect reverses in low unemployment environments.
failure_condition: In months where UNRATE > 8%, the monthly change in PAYEMS has a negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns, or the Rank IC over any 2-year window is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 800,000 units signal a severe downturn in residential construction, historically a leading indicator of recession, which depresses equity market valuation over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days after HOUST drops below 800, the SPX produces positive returns more than 30% of the time, or the mean return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales reflect strong consumer spending, which directly drives corporate revenue and earnings, pushing equity prices higher over short horizons.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return of SPX is not positively correlated with RSAFS level or its first difference over any 1-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: When strong GDP growth (quarter-on-quarter above 2%) coincides with a rising federal funds rate, the market interprets this as a deliberate policy tightening to curb overheating, which reduces expected future earnings and leads to negative long-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq growth > 2% and FEDFUNDS is higher than its value 3 months ago
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days after the joint trigger occurs, the SPX forward return is positive more than 35% of the time, or the average return is not negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is contracting year-over-year (yoy < 0%), any positive change in INDPRO signals a cyclical bottom and early recovery, which is strongly positive for equities. This signal is absent during normal growth.
failure_condition: In months where INDPRO yoy < 0%, the monthly change in INDPRO has a negative or flat relationship with 20-day forward SPX returns (Rank IC <= 0).
